page: Microsoft Azure EC4ads_cc_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 154 GB of local disk storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is weaker, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also rank in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and Geekbench text processing perform at average levels, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor, landing in the bottom 10%.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 and dedicated CPU allocation, the EC4ads_cc_v5 offers qualitative resource density for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The inclusion of 154 GB of local storage and Premium Storage support makes it suitable for small-scale databases, memory-bound caching layers, and text processing applications, though it is less optimal for highly parallelized compute tasks or high-throughput web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure EC4ads_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by a 3.525 GHz AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. While single-core CPU performance is strong, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are relatively weak. This configuration is best suited for memory-bound workloads, small databases, and text processing tasks.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, generous RAM-per-core density, and local storage for memory-bound workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
154 GB of local disk storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core performance contrasted by weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results
Well-suited for memory-bound applications, small databases, and text processing
